ab: Cilimaw kiso anini^ haw?
ch: M:你今天有空嘛?
en: Are you available today?

ab: O maan ko demak?
ch: W:怎麼了?
en: What is the matter?

ab: Mangalay kako a miliso’ i tisowanan.
ch: M:我想去拜訪你。
en: I would like to visit you.

ab: Awaay kako i loma’, cidemak kako. Cilaen to ri.
ch: W:我今天有事不在家。改天吧。
en: I am out today. How about another day?

ab: Cilimaw kiso anocila^ haw? Tayra kako i tisowanan i herek no lahok.
ch: M:那你明天有空嗎?我下午去找你。
en: Then, are you available tomorrow? I’ll visit you tomorrow afternoon.

ab: Nga’ay to, talaen ako kiso i loma’.
ch: W:可以，我在家等你。
en: Sure, I will wait for you at home.

ab: Manga’ay kako a milu-ko-ong haw? Mangalay kako a mikilok.
ch: M:請問我可以錄音嗎?我想做紀錄。
en: Would you mind if  I do the voice recording? I would like to keep a record.

ab: Manga’ay to! Mamaan!
ch: W:好啊!沒有問題!
en: Sure! No problem.

ab: Manga’ay kako a mi-lu-ing haw?
ch: M:可以錄影嗎?
en: Would you mind if I do the video recording?

ab: Caay ka salongan kako, caay haca^ ka solimet ko pa’aririan, hamaanen ri!
ch: W:我不好看，倉庫也沒整理，不要啦!
en: No, I am plain and the warehouse hasn’t been tidied up yet.

ab: Ano matira, sya-sing hato aca^ ako.
ch: M:那我拍照就好。
en: Then I will just take photos.

ab: Akaa pi-sya-sing.
ch: W:拍照也不要。
en: No photographing either.

ab: Aray ko pihayda iso i takowanan a miliso’.
ch: M:謝謝你讓我訪問。
en: Thank you for accepting the interview.

ab: Nga’ay to! O maan ko lalicayen iso?
ch: W:沒問題！你想問什麼？
en: You are welcome. What would you like to know?

ab: O maan ko tayal iso yo o limecedan ho^ kiso?
ch: M:請問您年輕時是做什麼的？
en: What do you do when you are young?

ab: O tayring i ’ayaw, nika misawad to kako a matayal.
ch: W:我以前是警察，但我現在退休了。
en: I was a police officer but I am retired now.

ab: Matayal ho^ kiso imatini haw?
ch: M:那你現在有工作嗎?
en: Then, are you working at the moment?

ab: I picodadan kako a matayal.
ch: W:我在學校上班。
en: I am working at school.

ab: Milicay haca^ kako, i niyaro’ ko wawa^ no miso a maro’ haw?
ch: M:請問您的孩子也住在部落嗎?
en: Do your kids live in the tribe too?

ab: Caay, i to-kay a maemin ko wawa^ ako a ma’orip.
ch: W:不，我的孩子在都市生活。
en: No, my kids are all living in the city.

ab: Ca:cay kiso a maro’ haw?
ch: M:那你自己一個人住嗎?
en: Then, do you live by yourself?

ab: Ciwaco^ ato ci’ayam kako. O malo paro^ no loma’ ako caira.
ch: W:我還有養一隻狗和一隻鳥，他們也是我的家人。
en: I also keep a dog and a bird. They are my family.

ab: Ciposi:^ kako, ira caira saka caay ka araaw.
ch: M:我也有養一隻貓，有動物陪伴不會無聊。
en: I have a cat too. I won’t get bored with the company of my pet.

ab: Mangalay kako a mitengil to pakayniay i paysin no pi’adop a demak.
ch: M:我想請問你關於打獵的禁忌。
en: I would like to ask you about the taboos of hunting.

ab: Caay ka adihay ko nikafana’ ako to pi’adop, nika ano o kafana’an ako i, ca’ofen ako kiso.
ch: W:我知道的不多，但我能回答的都會回答你。
en: I don’t know much about that, but I will tell you everything I know.

ab: Ira ko paysin a demak to pakayniay i sapi’adop a lalosidan haw?
ch: M:你們打獵的武器有什麼禁忌嗎?
en: Do you have any taboos about hunting weapons?

ab: O sapi’adop a lalosidan i, caay papitefing i tamiyanan o fafahiyan.
ch: W:我們打獵的武器不可以讓女生碰到。
en: Our hunting weapons are untouchable to women.

ab: Manga’ay a mihakelong ko fafahiyan a mi’adop haw?
ch: M:那女人可以一起去打獵嗎?
en: Then, can women go hunting together?

ab: Caay, caay ka nga’ay ko fafahiyan a mi’adop.
ch: W:不行，女人也不可以打獵。
en: No, women cannot go hunting either.
